decisions.decision: Preserve hybrid finger-skate product: two contacts as feet; click kick/pop; relative board-local control; Skate-like initiation + THUG2-like compact plaza lines. First ship expands grind family to include boardslide-class sideways entry; vertical slice may still land 50-50 first.
decisions.rationale: User intent and cycle-1 product fantasy survive adversarial review; grind expansion answers explicit sideways-into-grind desire without forcing boardslide into the smallest G1-G2 proof slice.
alternatives: Boardslide required in first vertical slice
rejectionReasons: Rigid micro-sim fails arcade line exploration
Canned menu fails continuous control fantasy
Boardslide in smallest slice raises grind entry complexity before feel proven
decisions.implementationConsequence: Prototype slice: push/steer/ollie/nollie/flip/shuv/catch/land/bail + 50-50; first ship: add boardslide family and broader plaza art after G2.
decisions.validationMethod: Playtest vocabulary checklist + G2 formative feel
decisions.reopenTrigger: G2 fails because grind family incomplete for enjoyment

decisions.decision: Adopt @dimforge/rapier3d-deterministic-compat@0.19.3 (Apache-2.0) as primary physics npm package with init-via-await RAPIER.init(); pin exact version at implement.
decisions.rationale: npm registry 2026-07-10 lists both @dimforge/rapier3d-deterministic and -compat at 0.19.3. Cycle 1 mixed bare name with -compat URL. Compat inlines WASM base64, better first-path for Vite/WebView2. Official docs state JS Rapier is cross-platform deterministic with caveats.
rejectionReasons: Separate WASM more bundler friction for first host
Non-labeled package adds golden-trace ambiguity
cannon-es weaker official determinism story
decisions.implementationConsequence: package.json depends on -compat 0.19.3; import path documented; upgrade requires re-running G4 golden hashes.
decisions.validationMethod: npm install + init smoke + world.createSnapshot hash on fixed steps
decisions.reopenTrigger: WASM payload size breaks packaging or init fails in WebView2

decisions.decision: First executable P0 spike implements BOTH paths, but production-primary ranking is Raw Input HID Touch Pad (0x0D/0x05) first for free dual-plant; Win11 RegisterTouchpadCapableWindow + GetPointerFrameTouchpadInfo is co-spike and preferred only if it proves free dual-plant on target hardware.
decisions.rationale: MS Learn documents registered windows receive WM_POINTER for two-finger pans and zooms; pre-release disclaimer remains. Free dual-plant for feet is not documented as guaranteed. Raw Input against PTP HID is the proven multi-contact class path (with study sample). Choose evidence over aesthetic preference for Win11 modern API.
alternatives: Win11 pointer primary with Raw fallback only
rejectionReasons: Pointer-as-primary risks months on pan/zoom-only stream
Raw-only skips free official path that may still work
decisions.implementationConsequence: P0 host binary must log both adapters; G1 accept can pass on either path; packaging uses the path that meets accept criteria.
decisions.validationMethod: P0 spike CSV traces with accept/reject metrics
decisions.reopenTrigger: Pointer path proves free dual-plant with lower latency than Raw on target device

decisions.decision: Keep report-level Button 1; attribute kick to left/right foot mental model via planted-state mapping (tail-only=ollie, nose-only=nollie, both=push default) plus motion windows. Device-mode matrix defines supported/ignored/rejected modes. Spatial click zone is advisory only, never sole authority.
decisions.rationale: PTP buttons are report-level, not per-contact (MS docs). User left/right foot model is reconstructed from stance + plant mask, not hardware finger identity.
alternatives: Use only Windows left/right click zones
rejectionReasons: Per-finger force not guaranteed
Click zones are OS UI concepts not feet
decisions.implementationConsequence: Device mode matrix in input-platform-and-device-spec; profile toggles for tap-to-click and bothClickMeans.
decisions.validationMethod: Hardware matrix tests + synthetic plant-mask golden traces
decisions.reopenTrigger: Hardware exposes reliable per-contact force usable without false pops

decisions.decision: v0: single dynamic board rigid body + cosmetic rider mass bias + assisted ManeuverSpec impulses. P3 probe: add constrained/raycast truck-wheel contacts if hull fails rail/lean. Reject full articulated multi-body rider for first ship.
decisions.rationale: Single body minimizes determinism risk and implementation cost while hybrid assist can open flip/grind windows. Rail fidelity may force raycast wheels later; full articulated system is high risk for little v0 gain.
rejectionReasons: Raycast day-one delays G2 feel proof
Articulated model high CPU and instability risk
decisions.implementationConsequence: BoardController applies impulses to one body; colliders: deck hull + optional truck boxes; grind as constraint/latch not full truck sim.
decisions.validationMethod: Physics comparison checklist + P3 stability metrics
decisions.reopenTrigger: Single body fails fair rail entry or off-axis land under G2

decisions.decision: Primary host: C# / .NET 8 + WebView2. Switch trigger to Rust: unresolved memory-safety defects in HID report parsing that C# cannot adequately contain, or team requirement for single Rust toolchain across host+tools.
decisions.rationale: WebView2Samples and Windows productivity favor C#. Rust is viable but dual-skill cost is real; switch only on evidence.
rejectionReasons: Rust-first delays WebView2 host without proven need
Electron-only is heavier packaging default
decisions.implementationConsequence: host/ project is .NET; native bridge emits ContactFrame JSON via PostWebMessageAsJson.
decisions.validationMethod: P0 host builds and posts frames into page
decisions.reopenTrigger: Documented HID safety failure class or packaging dead-end

decisions.decision: Default fixed physics step 60 Hz for development and first ship candidate. Benchmark 120 Hz as quality mode with same golden traces; adopt 120 only if step CPU p95 budget holds on target iGPU and formative feel improves.
decisions.rationale: Cycle 1 preferred 120 without device measurement. 60 is safer default; 120 is experiment not constant.
rejectionReasons: 120 unproven on iGPU
Variable dt breaks determinism goldens
decisions.implementationConsequence: SimConfig.hz=60 default; feature flag 120; render interpolates.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Measured 120 within budget and improves land/grind fairness

decisions.decision: Acquire CC0 HDRI + PBR materials + Kenney blockout with full provenance. Reject Kenney as final look. Hero board and shoes are bespoke Blender-ready gaps deferred because Blender process is owned by unrelated work (not because optional). No runtime/ promotions.
decisions.rationale: Codex audit P1: shell is insufficient. Free high-quality hero board/shoes with safe redistribution not found.
rejectionReasons: Kenney conflicts professional target
Sketchfab licenses often non-redistributable
decisions.implementationConsequence: assets/source/vendor populated; gap briefs in cycle-2; cycle 3 schedules isolated Blender pass.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Credible redistributable hero board/shoes appear with license page evidence

decisions.decision: Separate structural smoke, deterministic automated regression, hardware acceptance, formative feel (n small), tuning study, and release confidence. n>=5 is formative only, never release proof.
decisions.rationale: Codex audit P2 conflation of playtest n with ship quality.
rejectionReasons: Statistically weak for release
decisions.implementationConsequence: observability-and-verification.md tags every gate with evidence level.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Release process defines different statistical policy

decisions.decision: Autonomous agents may implement and test everything through ContactFrame injection. Must pause at G1 (device), G2 (human feel), and any hardware matrix item requiring physical trackpad. Failed G1 stops content production and pivots to input only.
decisions.rationale: Codex audit P1 autonomy must be gate-aware.
rejectionReasons: Spends months on wrong product after G1 fail
decisions.implementationConsequence: autonomy-and-gate-plan.md is binding for cycle 3 production goals.
decisions.validationMethod: Process review + agent harness contract tests
decisions.reopenTrigger: New remote device farm proves dual-contact without local human

decisions.decision: Boardslide family is first-ship required (sideways yaw into grind). Not required to pass first vertical-slice fun gate; 50-50 remains slice-first grind for simpler rail approach.
decisions.rationale: User wants turning sideways into grind; adversarial review keeps slice small but elevates boardslide to first ship.
rejectionReasons: Slice complexity risk
Defer conflicts explicit user intent for sideways grind
decisions.implementationConsequence: ManeuverSpec grind kinds include fifty_fifty and boardslide; slice milestone checklist marks boardslide post-G2.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Boardslide recognition costs too many false conflicts with flip yaw

decisions.decision: Disembodied shoes/feet with procedural attachment and catch assist radii; no full humanoid required for v0. Visual foot animation is cosmetic; catch uses generous board-local catch volumes not millimeter shoe mesh contact.
decisions.rationale: Players must not need impossible precision; shoes carry readability.
rejectionReasons: Humanoid cost high
No feet hurts foot-placement readability
decisions.implementationConsequence: Two shoe meshes parented/procedurally placed; catch volume != shoe mesh.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Playtests demand visible lower-leg for readability

decisions.decision: Implementable ManeuverAssist: state machine opens interruptible envelopes with target omega, impulse clamps, catch damping, landing cones, grind latch constraints; assist levels 0-2 scale snap and quantize only.
decisions.rationale: Cycle 1 hybrid concept needs math-enough spec for implementation without canned pose cheats.
rejectionReasons: Pure free too harsh for finger metaphor
Animation-only fails collision/fail fantasy
decisions.implementationConsequence: See physics-animation-and-camera-spec.md equations and state table.
decisions.reopenTrigger: Assist 1 still feels magnetic/unfair in G2
